prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [SILVER] Source: Degradation Study of the Peel Strength of Mini-Modules Under | Statement: Four damp heat conditions tested from 65C/85%RH baseline up to 95C/85%RH with peel tests at 90 degrees and 50 mm/min
2. [GOLD] Source: Influence of Hydrothermal Aging under Two Typical Adhesives  | Statement: Tensile strength of adhesive joints decreased about 31.3% after moisture absorption and 54.4% under combined high temperature 95C and humid environmen
3. [SILVER] Source: Influence of Hydrothermal Aging under Two Typical Adhesives  | Statement: Damp-heat aging of BFRP joints tested at 80C/95%RH for 0, 10, 20, and 30 days using epoxy adhesives Araldite 2015 and 2012
4. [SILVER] Source: Influence of Hydrothermal Aging under Two Typical Adhesives  | Statement: Moisture causes plasticization as free water fills pores and adsorbed water reacts with hydrophilic functional groups in the adhesive
5. [SILVER] Source: Influence of Hydrothermal Aging under Two Typical Adhesives  | Statement: Interface strength reduction, microstructure defects, and polymer matrix hydrolysis are irreversible effects of damp-heat aging
